# Data Commons

> A unified knowledge graph of public statistical data from the UN, World Bank, CDC, and more.

Data Commons is a platform for harmonizing public statistical data from hundreds of sources. It consists of a public website, datacommons.org, and a "custom" implementation run by third-parties to showcase their own data. The platform features natural-language querying, rich visualizations, downloadable data, APIs, and MCP integration.

## Core resources
- [Data Commons home](https://datacommons.org/): The main entry point for natural-language data exploration.
- [Documentation](https://docs.datacommons.org/): Comprehensive guides for users and developers.
- [Data sources](https://datacommons.org/data): Full list of providers and datasets integrated into the graph.
- [Custom Data Commons](https://docs.datacommons.org/custom_dc/): Instructions for hosting an instance of the platform.
- [Blog](https://blog.datacommons.org/): Release notes and launch announcements.

## Visualization tools
- [Timeline Explorer](https://datacommons.org/tools/visualization#visType=timeline): Visualize data trends over time for selected metrics.
- [Map Explorer](https://datacommons.org/tools/visualization#visType=map):
Visualize distributionss of data across geographic regions, for selected metrics.
- [Scatter Plot Explorer](https://datacommons.org/tools/visualization#visType=scatter):
Visualize the correlation between two metrics.
- [Web Components](https://docs.datacommons.org/api/web_components/): Custom elements for embedding Data Commons visualizations in your own website.

## Other tools
- [Data Download Tool](https://datacommons.org/tools/download): Download query results in CSV format.
- [Statistical Variable Explorer](https://datacommons.org/tools/statvar): Browse the available variables and metadata in the graph.
- [Knowledge Graph Browser](https://datacommons.org/browser/): Tool for inspecting individual entities and variables.

## APIs
- [REST API](https://docs.datacommons.org/api/rest/v2): Endpoints for querying nodes, triples, and observations.
- [Python API](https://docs.datacommons.org/api/python/v2): Library for programmatic data retrieval and analysis.
- [Colab tutorials](https://docs.datacommons.org/api/python/tutorials/): Python notebooks showing how to use Data Commons for data science.

## AI integration
- [Model Context Protocol (MCP)](https://docs.datacommons.org/mcp): Official server to connect LLM agents directly to Data Commons.

## Research & papers
- [DataGemma paper](https://docs.datacommons.org/papers/DataGemma-FullPaper.pdf): Details on grounding LLMs in Data Commons using RIG and RAG.
- [Technical overview](https://docs.datacommons.org/papers/dc_overview.pdf): Architecture of the knowledge graph and schema.
